title: Acetosella fruticula
taxonomy: Acetosella fruticula is classified within the family Oxalidaceae, commonly known as wood sorrel family. This classification places it among the flowering plants and specifically within the order Oxalidales. Further classification includes it being a member of the genus Acetosella, which is closely related to the genus Oxalis. It is native to Africa and is often found in rocky areas and woodlands. Its classification as a separate genus from Oxalis is based on differences in its leaf and flower morphology.

properties: Acetosella fruticula, also known as the succulent wood sorrel, is a plant with various medicinal properties. The plant's leaves are used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its therapeutic properties are attributed to the presence of compounds such as oxalic acid, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is also used to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and astringent properties. Additionally, it is believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress.
constituents: Acetosella fruticula, also known as the red-tinged or low-growing Acetosella, contains various active constituents. preparations: Acetosella fruticula, also known as the 'sour grass', is used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments. The leaves of the plant are often used to make teas and infusions, which are believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
